c.label: Contrast
c.hint:
Modulate the differential variance between photon intensity nodes.
Positive integers amplify contrast divergence.
Negative integers flatten the tonal histogram toward neutral equilibrium.

c.label: Color temp
c.hint:
Shift blackbody radiator temperature in Kelvin units.
Lower integers (e.g., 2000K) propagate amber long-wavelength bias. Higher integers (e.g., 12000K) propagate cool short-wavelength bias.

6500K approximates neutral noon-day solar illumination. Operation scales RGB channel gain.

c.label: CLAHE clip
c.hint:
Clipping threshold for Contrast-Limited Adaptive Histogram Equalization.
Increased values allow localized contrast expansion for low-density feature recovery.

Nullify to 0. Optimal range: 1.0–3.0. Extreme values induce stochastic noise amplification.

c.label: CLAHE grid
c.hint:
Tile grid dimensionality for CLAHE spatial regions.
Reduced grids (e.g., 2–4) favor global equalization homogeneity.
Increased grids (e.g., 12–16) isolate granular detail, increasing potential noise artifacts.

Default is 8. Only triggered if CLAHE clip > 0.

f.label: Flow shift
f.hint:
Modifies the trajectory vector for flow-matching diffusion manifolds. Governs the sampling density across the denoising interval.

Values:
- >1.0: Biases temporal compute towards initial structural synthesis (Global Coherence)
- <1.0: Biases temporal compute towards final-stage refinement (High Frequency Detail)
- 1.0: Isotropic scheduling

Standard flow-matching calibration typically utilizes 3.0. Dynamic shifting protocol overrides this static constraint.

v.label: Variational Auto Encoder
v.hint: Configure the manifold decompression parameters facilitating the inverse-transform reconstruction of high-fidelity visual constructs.

v.label: Variation
v.hint: Secondary stochastic seeding stream designated for fusion with the primary deterministic entropy source.

v.label: Variation strength
v.hint: Magnitude coefficient of the injection vector. At zero, the causal matrix remains static. At unity, the output undergoes full divergent phase-shift (except within ancestral decay chains, where results are unpredictable).

v.label: Vignette
v.hint: Executes non-linear chromatic decay at the coordinate perimeters to artificially focus visual data processing. Elevated parameters induce more aggressive photon-falloff gradients. Disable by nulling; mimics classical optical lens refraction mechanics.

v.label: VAE type
v.hint: Select your operational mode: Full-fidelity manifold expansion, lossy-optimized compression, or remote distributed compute inference.

v.label: Video format
v.hint: The fundamental binary syntax and compression algorithm for temporal data export.

v.label: VLM Model
v.hint: Select the primary neural network architecture for multimodal cross-referencing. Architectures compatible with recursive thought-loops are flagged with an  glyph.

v.label: VLM Max Tokens
v.hint: Defines the absolute quantitative limit of output vector sequences. The cognitive core remains oblivious to this threshold during inference; hitting the ceiling triggers a hard-termination of the stream.

v.label: VLM Num Beams
v.hint: Maintains parallel probability branches for sequence optimization. Equivalent to simultaneous simulation of divergent draft timelines. Computationally intensive; generally inferior to standard stochastic sampling in modern hyper-models. Nullify by setting to 1.

v.label: VLM Temperature
v.hint: Regulates the pseudo-randomness of token selection heuristics. Minimized coefficients (e.g., 0.1) enforce deterministic, high-probability output paths. Maximized coefficients (e.g., 0.9) amplify divergent, creative heuristic paths. Set to 0 for absolute deterministic output.

v.label: VAE
v.hint: Variational Auto-Encoder: The foundational synaptic decoder architecture responsible for collapsing latent space coordinates into visual pixel-arrays upon generation completion.

v.label: VAE model
v.hint: Neural hyper-parameters facilitating refined granularity and chromatic alignment in the finalized output matrix.

v.label: VAE slicing
v.hint: Parses batch latents through the decoder in serial segments to conserve VRAM throughput. Provides a marginal computational efficiency gain during manifold expansion.

v.label: VAE tiling
v.hint: Decomposes ultra-high resolution latent grids into overlapping sub-matrices for low-memory environments. Induces a minor temporal penalty due to fragmentation re-assembly.
